meta.title: {brand.holdType} — Mac dictation with GPT-4o Transcribe
meta.description: {brand.holdType} is native {brand.macOS} dictation with GPT-4o Transcribe. Use your own {brand.openAI} API key, with no {brand.holdType} subscription or mandatory model rewrite.

localeUi.suggestionMessage: This page is available in English.

hero.title: Native Mac dictation with GPT-4o Transcribe.
hero.support: Hold the shortcut in a {brand.mac} app, speak, and release. {brand.holdType} sends the recording to GPT-4o Transcribe through your {brand.openAI} account and puts the transcript at the cursor in most {brand.mac} apps. Model-based correction is optional and off by default.
hero.qualification: Free app · {brand.openAI} API usage billed separately · {fact.minimumOS}

price.aria: At the current estimated transcription rate, {pricing.dailyDictations} short dictations cost about {pricing.dailyPrice}.

demo.intro: Refactor the upload path without changing user-visible behavior.
demo.transcribingBody: Sending the recording to GPT-4o Transcribe…
demo.insertedBody: Preserve retry state, keep the request timeout bounded, and add a regression test for the failed upload path.
demo.caption: Illustrated {brand.holdType} workflow using a sample technical dictation. This is not a recorded demo.

workflow.title: Hold. Speak. Release. The transcript appears at the cursor.

inserted.title: Inserted
inserted.body: The transcript appears at the cursor

stays.title: GPT-4o Transcribe by default
stays.body: The configurable default is GPT-4o Transcribe, not whisper-1. Change it in Settings when another OpenAI transcription model is needed.

subscription.title: Pay OpenAI for usage
subscription.body: {brand.holdType} adds no monthly fee. {brand.openAI} deducts API usage from the {brand.openAIPlatform} account connected through your key.

openAI.title: No mandatory second {brand.openAI} pass
openAI.body: Model-based correction is optional and off by default. Local typography cleanup can run without another model request.

useCases.title: Dictate work—not voice memos to sort out later.
useCases.intro: {brand.holdType} is for text that belongs in an active app now: a coding task, a message, a document, or a translated reply.

aiPrompts.title: Technical prompts
aiPrompts.body: Dictate a {brand.codex}, {brand.claude}, or {brand.chatGPT} request with file names, constraints, and acceptance criteria instead of assembling it line by line.

everyday.title: Messages and working documents
everyday.body: Dictate mail, reviews, documentation, chats, and notes directly at the cursor instead of moving a draft through another editor.

bilingual.title: Dictate and translate
bilingual.body: Set a target language, then use the dedicated shortcut to transcribe speech and insert the translation.

features.appUiLanguageNote: The current {brand.holdType} app and the product screenshots use an English interface.

translation.label: Language and vocabulary
translation.title: Translate only when you ask for it.
translation.body1: Choose a target language, then use {keyboard.translationShortcut}. Translation runs as a separate text request after transcription.
translation.body2: {appUi.localDictionary} entries add spelling context for names, project terms, and file names to the transcription request.
translation.imageAlt: {brand.holdType} {appUi.translation} settings with the translation shortcut and target language controls
translation.caption: Real {brand.holdType} {appUi.translation} settings. On phones, swipe the focused crop horizontally to inspect it.

menu.imageAlt: {brand.holdType} menu bar popover with transcribe, translate, {appUi.pasteLastResult}, history, settings, and quit actions
menu.imageCaption: Real menu bar controls, including {appUi.lastResult} recovery.
menu.label: Recovery and control
menu.title: Keep the transcript when insertion fails.
menu.body1: If insertion fails, the accepted transcript remains available as {appUi.lastResult}. Paste it with {keyboard.pasteShortcut} without replacing the {brand.macOS} system clipboard.
menu.body2: Model-based {brand.openAI} correction is off by default. The menu bar keeps dictation, translation, recovery, history, and settings together.

privacy.label: Cost, requests, and storage
privacy.title: Audio goes to {brand.openAI}. HoldType runs no product backend.
privacy.intro: {brand.holdType} has no account system, product backend, telemetry, analytics, or cloud sync. Transcription itself is an {brand.openAI} API request made with the key you configure.

dataPath.title: Where the key, audio, and text go

items.apiKey: The API key is stored locally in {brand.macOSKeychain}.
items.audio: Audio is sent to {brand.openAI} for transcription.
items.optionalTextRequests: Optional correction and translation are separate text requests.
items.failedAudio: Completed audio is not retained by default. Recoverable failures may keep bounded session-only audio for {appUi.retry}.
items.recordingCache: Optional recording-cache retention is local and user-controlled.

billing.title: Pay OpenAI for transcription—not HoldType for access
billing.body: {brand.holdType} has no subscription. {brand.openAI} deducts actual API usage from the {brand.openAIPlatform} account connected through your key; {brand.holdType} does not collect a monthly fee.
billing.exampleBody: {pricing.dailyDictations} short dictations represent roughly {pricing.recordedMinutes} minutes of recorded speech. Repeat that amount for {pricing.daysInMonth} days and the estimated transcription cost is about {pricing.monthlyPrice}.
billing.note: The {appUi.billing} screen is a local estimate for successful audio transcriptions. It is not an {brand.openAI} invoice and does not include correction or translation requests.
billing.imageAlt: {brand.holdType} {appUi.billing} settings showing a local transcription usage estimate
billing.imageCaption: On phones, swipe the focused crop of the same real {appUi.billing} screenshot horizontally.

founder.imageAlt: A desk microphone used while building and testing {brand.holdType}
founder.imageCaption: Special recording hardware is not required.
founder.label: Why I built {brand.holdType}
founder.title: I wanted the transcript, not another subscription or rewrite pipeline.
founder.body1: I had tried Wispr Flow, OpenWhispr, Codex voice input, and local Whisper setups. I did not want another platform around dictation. I wanted a small native app with one job.
founder.body2: In my daily work, {brand.openAI} transcription gave me the first pass I trusted most often. That is why GPT-4o Transcribe is the default and model-based correction stays off until I enable it.
founder.body3: The app is native Swift, works through the user’s API key, and adds no account or monthly fee. {brand.holdType} is built and tested through the same voice workflow I use to direct Codex.

steps.download: Download the latest disk image from {brand.githubReleases}.
steps.permissions: Grant microphone, global-shortcut, and insertion permissions.
steps.apiKey: Add your {brand.openAIPlatform} API key in Settings.

homebrew.title: Prefer {brand.homebrew}?
homebrew.body: The project tap installs the same disk image published on {brand.githubReleases}.
homebrew.copyFailureStatus: Copy failed. Select the command manually.

apiKeyGuide.label: New to API keys?
apiKeyGuide.title: Create an API key, then add it in Settings.
apiKeyGuide.intro: An API key is a private credential that lets {brand.holdType} send audio for transcription through your {brand.openAIPlatform} account. It is separate from your {brand.chatGPT} login and subscription.

parts.value: Sign in to {brand.openAIPlatform} and open the

steps.create: Create a secret key and copy it when {brand.openAI} shows it.
steps.save: In {brand.holdType}, open {appUi.settingsOpenAI}, paste the key, and save.

apiKeyGuide.note: {brand.openAI} shows the full key only when it is created. Keep it private and paste it only into {brand.holdType}, which stores it locally in {brand.macOSKeychain}. API billing is separate from {brand.chatGPT}, and a new {brand.openAIPlatform} account may require prepaid credit.

video.iframeTitle: How to create a {brand.chatGPT} API key ({video.year} tutorial)
video.title: See how an {brand.openAI} API key is created
video.noScriptAction: Watch the API key tutorial on {brand.youtube}

parts.value: , {video.duration}, published June {video.year}. {brand.youtube} loads only after Play.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: Short answers to the decisions that matter before installing.

apiKeyRequired.question: Why is an {brand.openAI} API key required?

parts.value: {brand.holdType} sends transcription requests through your own {brand.openAIPlatform} account. The key is stored locally in {brand.macOSKeychain}. If API keys are new to you, follow the

chatGPTPlus.question: Is {brand.chatGPTPlus} enough?
chatGPTPlus.answer: No. {brand.chatGPT} subscriptions and {brand.openAIPlatform} API billing are separate. {brand.holdType} needs an API key with {brand.openAIPlatform} billing configured.

cost.question: What does dictation cost?
cost.answer: {brand.holdType} has no fixed fee. {brand.openAI} deducts API usage from your {brand.openAIPlatform} balance, and new API accounts may require prepaid credit. {brand.holdType}’s {appUi.billing} screen estimates successful transcriptions on this {brand.mac}; optional correction and translation are separate requests.

dataSent.question: What data is sent to {brand.openAI}?
dataSent.answer: Audio is sent for transcription. Optional correction and translation send transcript text in separate requests, and optional nearby context sends a short excerpt with the transcription request.

audioStorage.question: Is audio stored?
audioStorage.answer: Completed audio is not retained by default. A recoverable failure may keep bounded session-only audio for {appUi.retry}, and optional recording-cache retention is local and user-controlled.

compatibleApps.question: Which {brand.mac} apps work?
compatibleApps.answer: {brand.holdType} inserts accepted text in most {brand.mac} apps through {brand.macOS} Accessibility support. If handoff fails, {appUi.lastResult} remains available for recovery.

languages.question: Which languages are supported?
languages.answer: Transcription can use automatic or fixed source-language settings. Translation uses the target language you configure in {brand.holdType}.

permissions.question: Why does {brand.holdType} need {brand.macOS} permissions?
permissions.answer: Microphone access records speech, Input Monitoring supports the global shortcut, and Accessibility enables text insertion into the active app.

sourceAvailable.question: Is {brand.holdType} open source?
sourceAvailable.answer: {brand.holdType} is source-available under {license.fsl11} with an {license.mit} future license. Each released version converts to {license.mit} two years after release.

finalCta.title: Dictate with GPT-4o Transcribe on your Mac.
finalCta.body: Free app · No {brand.holdType} subscription · {brand.openAI} usage billed directly · {fact.minimumOS}

footer.description: Native {brand.macOS} dictation with GPT-4o Transcribe. Source-available under {license.fsl11} with an {license.mit} future license.
